obligations.reason: Visible JTAG/DTM ports, debug request handshakes, jtag_hash/jtag_unlock, debug_req_o, and dm_slave/dm_master signals indicate an externally controlled debug subject that must be mediated before reaching protected debug capabilities or bus transactions.
obligations.subject: external JTAG/DTM or simulation debug requester
obligations.operation: trigger debug request and issue debug read/write transactions
obligations.object: hart debug control and debug module bus-access signals
obligations.expected_guard: debug authorization or jtag_unlock/authentication state before any debug request can control hart debug entry or access debug-module/memory-facing transactions
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/DTM inputs -> dmi_jtag/SimJTAG/Debug Module request path -> debug_req_o / dm_slave_* / dm_master_*
state_conditions_to_check: jtag_unlock derivation from jtag_hash or auth comparison
whether testmode_i or simulation JTAG path changes guard requirements
reset state of debug authorization
whether dm_master is disabled or still reachable
required_evidence_slots: subject: tck_i/tms_i/trst_ni/td_i or SimJTAG/DTM requester
operation: debug request, DMI read/write, hart debug entry, bus read/write
object: debug_req_o, dm_slave_* or dm_master_*
expected guard: jtag_unlock/debug authorization/debug_mode policy
observed RTL condition on request path
source locations for assignments/instances/connections
knowledge_used: evidence closure: subject, operation, object, guard, reachability
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows relevant signals and instances but not all connections/guards; inspect visible RTL around lines 233-351 and debug instantiations.

obligations.reason: reglk_wrapper exposes a software-visible AXI path and jtag_unlock input producing a lock-control vector used by wrappers; lock bits are protected assets only if all updates and clears are mediated and sticky where required.
obligations.subject: software-visible AXI requester, with possible JTAG unlock override
obligations.operation: write, clear, or update lock bits
obligations.object: reglk_ctrl_o/reglk_ctrl lock vector controlling peripheral register writes
obligations.expected_guard: writes that set lock state should be sticky or only modifiable under an authorized unlock condition; clears/overwrites after lock should require jtag_unlock or equivalent privileged authorization
obligations.candidate_path: reglk_wrapper AXI register write path -> reglk_ctrl_o -> reglk_ctrl consumers in peripheral wrappers
state_conditions_to_check: reset value of reglk_ctrl
whether writing 0 can clear previously set lock bits
whether jtag_unlock bypass permits intended unlock only
whether rst_9 or rst_ni can clear locks during operation
required_evidence_slots: subject: AXI register interface requester
operation: write/clear lock bits
object: reglk_ctrl_o and internal reglk_ctrl registers
expected guard: prior lock state plus jtag_unlock/privilege/accounting gate
observed write enable/address decode/data update logic
source locations for update and output assignments
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect sequential block around reglk_wrapper line ~86 and top-level connections.

obligations.reason: The fact layer shows acc_ctrl as a central access-control matrix and at least one use site `rom_req = rom_req_acct && acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0]`; policy register update and all use sites must be checked for consistent mediation.
obligations.subject: software-visible AXI requester and current priv_lvl_i requester
obligations.operation: configure access policy and gate peripheral requests
obligations.object: acc_ctrl/acc_ctrl_o access-control matrix and gated peripheral request enables such as rom_req
obligations.expected_guard: access-control policy updates should be writable only by authorized privilege/locked configuration, and request gating should consistently require acc_ctrl_c indexed by the current requester privilege for each protected peripheral
obligations.candidate_path: AXI/NOC requester -> acct_wrapper -> acc_ctrl_o -> acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral] gating of peripheral requests
state_conditions_to_check: reset/default value of acc_ctrl
whether lower privilege can grant itself access
whether reglk_ctrl_i protects acct writes
whether acc_ctrl_c alias expression for j==5 and j==4 is intended and safe
whether all peripheral paths use acc_ctrl consistently
required_evidence_slots: subject: AXI/MMIO requester writing acct registers
operation: policy write and peripheral request
object: acc_ctrl bits and per-peripheral request enables
expected guard: privilege/lock guard for policy writes and acc_ctrl_c use for accesses
observed write enable/address decode and request assignments
source locations for update logic and use sites
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer includes one explicit use site and wrapper ports; inspect full riscv_peripherals for all acc_ctrl_c consumers.

obligations.reason: pkt_wrapper has fuse_req_o, fuse_addr_o, fuse_rdata_i and pkey_loc, which are protected/secret-like assets; check that AXI reads cannot directly expose fuse contents without policy guard.
obligations.subject: software-visible AXI/NOC requester
obligations.operation: request and read fuse or key-location data
obligations.object: fuse interface data and addresses, including fuse_rdata_i/fuse_rdata and pkey_loc
obligations.expected_guard: fuse reads and fuse address selection should require an authorized access policy/lock/privilege condition and should not expose fuse data to unauthorized AXI/NOC requesters
obligations.candidate_path: pkt_wrapper AXI register path -> fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o -> fuse_rdata_i -> rdata/AXI response
state_conditions_to_check: reset/default of fuse-related registers
whether fuse_rdata is latched and remains readable after authorization changes
whether read and write enables are separately guarded
whether acct_ctrl_i and reglk_ctrl_i actually affect fuse_req or rdata
required_evidence_slots: subject: AXI/NOC requester to pkt_wrapper
operation: fuse request/read and AXI read response
object: fuse_rdata_i/fuse_rdata, fuse_addr_o, pkey_loc, rdata
expected guard: acct_ctrl_i/reglk_ctrl_i/privilege or lock condition
observed address decode, read mux, and fuse_req generation
source locations for fuse path and response path
obligations.uncertainty: Only interface-level facts are visible; inspect pkt_wrapper body around lines 38-80 and top-level fuse wiring.

obligations.reason: DMA can act as an independent bus master; visible configuration registers and lock/control inputs require checks that unprivileged software cannot program arbitrary protected memory/peripheral accesses or change config during active transfer.
obligations.subject: software-visible AXI requester and DMA engine as bus master
obligations.object: DMA configuration registers start_reg, length_reg, source_addr_*_reg, dest_addr_*_reg, core_lock_reg and transfer access
obligations.expected_guard: DMA start, source/destination address, length, and lock/control registers should be writable only when authorized and should enforce address-range/access-control checks for the DMA subject before active transfer
obligations.candidate_path: dma_wrapper AXI configuration registers -> DMA engine control/address/length -> memory or NOC transfer
state_conditions_to_check: reset values of config/start/done/valid registers
whether config writes are blocked when core_lock_reg or active transfer is set
whether source and destination address ranges are checked
whether start can be asserted with stale unauthorized addresses
whether rst_8 can reset locks or active state
required_evidence_slots: subject: AXI requester writing DMA wrapper
operation: write config/start and perform transfer
object: DMA source/destination/length/core lock registers and downstream bus transaction
expected guard: acct_ctrl_i/reglk_ctrl_i plus address range/PMP-like check
observed write enables/address decode/start logic
source locations for config update and transfer outputs
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists registers but not downstream DMA engine wiring; inspect dma_wrapper around line ~129 and top-level NOC/DMA connections.

obligations.reason: The top-level exposes many NOC ports for crypto and control peripherals plus acc_ctrl_c gating; obligation is to verify all security-sensitive request enables are mediated, not just boot ROM.
obligations.subject: CPU/NOC/AXI requester
obligations.operation: route reads/writes to peripherals over NOC/AXI
obligations.object: peripheral request/response paths and protected accelerator/control registers
obligations.expected_guard: each peripheral request should be gated by the access-control matrix or an equivalent address/privilege guard; security-sensitive peripherals should not have ungated NOC request or response paths
obligations.candidate_path: top-level NOC/AXI peripheral request routing -> crypto/security peripherals (AES0/1/2, RSA, RNG, SHA256, HMAC, RST, DEBUG, BOOTROM, CLINT, PLIC, PKT, ACCT, REGLK, DMA)
state_conditions_to_check: whether response data can be returned when request was denied
whether read and write paths use same guard consistently
whether default acc_ctrl permits all access after reset
whether debug/test paths bypass NOC gating
required_evidence_slots: subject: NOC/AXI requester with priv_lvl_i
operation: peripheral read/write routing
object: request valid/ready and response data for each peripheral
expected guard: acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral] or equivalent
observed request assignment/decode and response mux behavior
source locations for each gated/ungated path
obligations.uncertainty: Assignments are truncated; inspect riscv_peripherals full request generation and response mux sections.

obligations.reason: Top-level ports named we_flag_* and wrapper we_flag inputs may be intended test/override controls; verify they cannot force writes to protected registers without the normal lock/accounting guards.
obligations.subject: external/top-level write-enable flag source
obligations.object: protected configuration/control registers in wrappers, especially acc_ctrl and DMA registers
obligations.expected_guard: external or top-level write-enable override flags should not bypass reglk_ctrl/acct_ctrl/privilege checks for protected configuration registers
obligations.candidate_path: write-enable flags we_flag_0..we_flag_4 and wrapper we_flag inputs -> protected register update logic
state_conditions_to_check: whether we_flag is active during normal operation
whether testmode_i qualifies we_flag usage
whether we_flag can update registers while locked
reset behavior of registers affected by we_flag
required_evidence_slots: subject: external we_flag input or test/control source
operation: force write enable
object: acc_ctrl/DMA or other protected registers
expected guard: reglk_ctrl/acct_ctrl/privilege/test authorization
observed logic combining we_flag with normal we/address decode
source locations for we_flag connections and update conditions
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows ports but limited assignment data; inspect connections from riscv_peripherals to wrappers and wrapper write conditions.

obligations.reason: Multiple per-peripheral reset signals feed wrappers containing access-control, lock, DMA, and fuse paths; reset generation and effects must be checked for unauthorized policy clearing or unsafe residue.
obligations.subject: software/NOC/external reset controller path
obligations.object: lock/access-control/DMA/fuse-related wrapper state affected by rst_*
obligations.expected_guard: software- or externally-controlled resets of security wrappers should be authorized and should not clear locks/access policy or leave stale secret/control data observable in an unsafe state
obligations.candidate_path: peripheral-specific reset controls rst_1..rst_13 -> wrapper reset inputs rst_5/rst_6/rst_8/rst_9 and protected state
state_conditions_to_check: reset default of lock and access-control state
whether local resets differ from global rst_ni
whether reset can be triggered through unguarded MMIO/NOC writes
whether rdata/output registers are cleared on reset
required_evidence_slots: subject: requester or logic controlling rst_*
operation: assert reset/clear state
object: reglk_ctrl, acc_ctrl, DMA config/status, fuse-related state
expected guard: privileged reset authorization/lifecycle condition
observed reset generation and sequential reset effects
source locations for reset generation and reset branches
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect top-level reset-generation logic around rst_1..rst_13 declarations and wrapper sequential blocks.
